Troubleshoot / Update Imported File
Choose Source + Sheet
Click a row’s “Use Row” button to preview that row as headers. Click any cell to declare that cell as a column header if the row preview missed it.
Headers Found in Selected Row
Declare Missing Header From Cell
Representative Name Mapping
Use this when a file separates first/last name or stores full names as “Last, First.” The normalized representative name is used for matching and team assignments.
Team + Date Mapping
Use this when a file identifies representatives but does not reliably identify their coach/team. The rows will still match by representative name and inherit coach/team from trusted imported sources.
Date is checked automatically when a date-like column is found, but HireDate / Hire Date is ignored. Turn it off for roster or static team files.

This checks saved models against the headers from the files currently imported in this session. Pick the matching header in a row and apply it to update the saved model.

Teams to Include0 selected
Org Filters0 orgs selected · 0 coaches · 0 reps covered
Include orgs
Exclude orgs
Selected teams and included orgs are combined: a coach/team may enter the run if it is selected in Teams to Include or belongs to an included org; excluded orgs are removed.
Organizations to Run Separately0 selected
Choose two or more organizations. Every organization will use the same model, dates, view, QA settings, no-score setting, and saved PDF options.
Multi runs are sequential: generate → export PDF → generate → export PDF. Each PDF filename starts with its organization name.
Qualtrics Coaching Email Reports
Opening this section restores saved rules and connects 90-day KPI, Documented Coaching, and Checklist data once. Weekly Stats only load when you press the weekly button. No preview or report runs automatically.

Index not built Build tables and charts from imported Retail, Referral, QA, Checklist, Documented Coaching, Comp Calls, and model criteria results.
Representative List Tester
Paste Representative Names
Names may also be separated by tabs or semicolons. The tester checks imported associate-name data, Control rosters, saved representative aliases, and the representative-to-coach map.
Check Item Scan
Optionally count matching stored records for every pasted representative.
Select a stored source and header. Multiple phrases use the same quoted format: "phrase one", "phrase two". A record counts when it contains any entered phrase.
Unique Coaches
Paste names and press Test List.

Matched Record Details
Research Item
Guided Research BuilderBuild the question in plain language. The advanced calculation and join settings are generated for you and remain available below.
Live question summary
Choose what each result represents to begin.
Start with a common question
1What should each result represent?
2What do you want to know?
3Where should the tool look for evidence?
The first selected source is the subject population. Other selected sources are connected through mapped representative, coach, team, organization, and date fields.
Evaluate Representatives using evidence from the selected sources.
4What conditions must be true?
Conditions decide who or what qualifies. Phrase values are entered directly in the row and may contain one or more comma- or semicolon-separated phrases.
Advanced source, date, and join settings
Controls what one result represents.
Representative research never silently absorbs a full team.
5Limit the Population Filters
Filters restrict the people or records examined before the calculation. Use Team is for searchable multi-team or $organization selection; choose Exclude to include all groups except the selected groups.
6How should the answer be divided and displayed?
Changing the display never changes the calculation.
Advanced calculation and output settings
For line graphs, Multi add creates separate lines when no secondary series field is selected.
Choose table metrics or conversation display columns.
Visual Options
Advanced Calculation and Join Settings
When the same normalized representative name appears more than once, keep the version with the most linked data and exclude weaker duplicates from research calculations.
Type a column, expression, row['Column'], or !Sheet.Column.
Calculation Preview
Complete the guided question to verify the calculation.
